http.server/BaseHTTPRequestHandler/path.name: path
http.server/BaseHTTPRequestHandler/path.description: Contains the request path. If query component of the URL is present, then path includes the query. Using the terminology of RFC 3986, path here includes hier-part and the query.

info.excerpt: Contains the request path. If query component of the URL is present, then path includes the query. Using the terminology of RFC 3986, path here includes hier-part and the query.

http.server/BaseHTTPRequestHandler/proto.name: protocol_version
http.server/BaseHTTPRequestHandler/proto.description: Specifies the HTTP version to which the server is conformant. It is sent in responses to let the client know the server’s communication capabilities for future requests. If set to 'HTTP/1.1', the server will permit HTTP persistent connections; however, your server must then include an accurate Content-Length header (using send_header()) in all of its responses to clients. For backwards compatibility, the setting defaults to 'HTTP/1.0'.
